label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out which of the two options is sarcastic. Let's start by understanding what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is a form of irony where someone says the opposite of what they really mean, often to express contempt or mock a situation. It's usually said in a mocking or scornful tone. Let me look at the options again. Option A: He's just kindhearted. Okay, well nothing wrong with him running the country in that case! Option B: He's just demented. Okay, well nothing wrong with him
